user_query:
We need a practical reorder plan for three SKUs in our DTC shop. Please read the inputs in input/ and produce structured outputs in output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/sku_params.csv — columns: sku, current_stock, avg_daily_demand, demand_sd, lead_time_days, lead_time_sd, service_level, moq, pack_size, review_cycle_days
- input/assumptions.yaml — global defaults (e.g., service-level mapping, rounding rules, seasonality notes)
- input/promo_topics.json — promo-related topics to inform demand uplift (e.g., {"Miku Hoodie": {"topic": "Hatsune Miku"}})

Task:
1) For each SKU, calculate:
- lead time demand = avg_daily_demand × lead_time_days
- safety stock based on demand and lead-time variability and the service level target
- reorder point = lead time demand + safety stock
- recommended order quantity that covers the review cycle and respects MOQ and pack-size constraints (round up to the nearest pack multiple; if MOQ applies, ensure the final quantity meets or exceeds it). Explicitly separate "reorder point" (when to order) from "recommended order quantity" (how much to order).
2) Apply (and clearly document) any promo uplift assumptions. For Miku Hoodie, use the promo topic to inform a justified uplift factor; include the factor chosen in the assumptions.
3) Provide risk notes for each SKU (stockout risk, overstock/cash drag risk, and assumption sensitivity).
4) Produce the following files:
- output/reorder_report.json — a readable JSON report with, for each SKU, sections: assumptions, model (lead_time_demand, safety_stock, reorder_point), recommendation (suggested_order_qty, post_reorder_coverage_days, rounding_note), risk (stockout_risk_level, overstock_risk_level, notes). Include an overall summary at the top with date/time generated and any global assumptions used.
- output/reorder_report_min.json — a minified JSON version of the same data.
- output/reorder_report.yaml — a YAML conversion of the same data structure.
- output/validation.json — a short JSON indicating whether output/reorder_report.json is valid JSON syntax and any issues found.
- output/promo_justification.md — a short English summary (80–400 words) of the promo topic for Miku Hoodie and why a demand uplift is reasonable. Clearly indicate it is a summary, include a visible source link to Moegirl Wiki for the topic, and include the CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 CN license note.
- output/formula_sources.md — cite at least one official documentation page explaining how to map service level to a z-score or a relevant function commonly used to compute it (e.g., an Excel function page). Include a learn.microsoft.com link and briefly explain how you used it.

Make sure the numbers and constraints are plausible, and call out fragile assumptions. If anything is missing or unclear in the inputs, state the assumption explicitly in the report.
